mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS path lowers discount rates and signals easier liquidity conditions, which supports higher S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is confirmed when rate cuts persist across consecutive observations and fails if equities do not rise after sustained easing.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 60% of periods when FEDFUNDS is declining for at least two consecutive monthly observations, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS rises above a restrictive threshold, financial conditions tighten, equity valuation multiples compress, and forward S&P 500 returns turn negative over the next month. The mechanism is falsified if post-threshold rate hikes are followed by positive equity drift.
failure_condition: Reject if, after FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5%, the next 20-trading-day SPX return is positive in at least 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE reflects weakening household income and demand, which should pressure earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the following month. The signal is strongest when unemployment is broad-based rather than a one-off print.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with a 0.3 percentage point or larger 3-month UNRATE rise do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent payroll growth and declining unemployment indicate labor-market resilience, supporting future consumption and earnings and lifting S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect requires both employment measures to improve together; either series alone is insufficient.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with positive PAYEMS momentum and falling UNRATE do not outperform unconditional 60-day SPX returns, or if either variable alone explains the same effect without interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A broad decline in RSAFS indicates weakening nominal consumer spending, which should reduce revenue expectations and weigh on equity returns over the next month. The signal is validated when sales growth decelerates across multiple monthly readings and invalidated if weak sales coincide with positive stock performance.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth turns negative on a 3-month annualized basis
failure_condition: Reject if negative 3-month annualized RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with stable inflation signals improving real activity without overheating, which should support equities through better earnings and margins over the next quarter. The mechanism fails if industrial production strengthens while returns systematically fall.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO momentum combined with low CPIAUCSL inflation does not outperform baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the return sign is not predominantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A housing downturn signaled by falling HOUST points to weaker construction activity, lower housing wealth effects, and softer cyclical demand, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when housing starts contract for several months in a row.
failure_condition: Reject if three-month streaks of declining HOUST are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth slows while CPIAUCSL remains elevated, the market faces stagflation risk: weaker real activity with sticky prices compresses real earnings expectations and raises discount-rate uncertainty, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The hypothesis fails if slow growth and sticky inflation do not jointly hurt equities.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 1.5% annualized and CPIAUCSL inflation > 3%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered stagflation episodes do not produce negative 60-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the effect disappears when either GDPC1 or CPIAUCSL is removed.
